Off Calendar May-15-2018 Denied

Atty. James J. Ficenec of Archer Norris, Tel. No. 1-925-930-6600 appearing for the
Plaintiff;
Atty. Scott L. Johnson, Tel. No. 1-415-846-0120 appearing for the Defendants.

Ruling - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Bill Leung, Lew Fong, Leung, Lee & Chan's motion to strike the entirety of the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Kinson Wong is denied. While Mr. Wong should have filed his first amended complaint within the time frame set by the court, his failure to do so does not warrant the harsh consequence of precluding him from pursuing the claims alleged in the first amended complaint, especially since defendants have not identified any legal prejudice to them from the late filing. Denying the motion comports with California's strong policy in favor of deciding cases on their merits. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF KINSON WONG'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint is granted. The trial date is continued to December 3, 2018 and all discovery deadlines are triggered off the new trial date. Mr. Wong is granted leave to file his proposed first amended complaint and he may include any additional allegations he believes is necessary regarding demand futility. The first amended complaint must be filed no later than February 9, 2018. Defendants may, if they wish, file a demurrer or motion to strike regarding any claims or allegations not alleged in the complaint. Granting Mr. Wong's motion comports with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings at any stage in a case. Any legal prejudice to defendants by the granting of the motion is eliminated by continuing the trial date and discovery deadlines.

Counsel for Mr. Wong is required to prepare a proposed order which is entitled "Order Continuing Trial Date and Granting Leave to File a First Amended Complaint" and repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
